contractSurface.securityClassification: platform-level feature registry, feature-porting decisions, adapter generation, simulator checks, and tenant-visible read-only feature inventory
contractSurface.identityNote: FLOW-36 uses feature-registry as the canonical portability, UI, package, and runtime implementation slug.
coreGuarantees: FT records keep stable feature identity across platforms.
portingCandidate is MACHINE-classified and cannot be overridden by tenant configuration.
Engine-internal features produce a terminal PROHIBITED result before cost estimation or adapter generation.
Feature-manifest schema v2.0 validation runs before FT records are committed.
FT-ID uniqueness is enforced before creating a registry entry.
Signal aggregation routes MODE_A or MODE_B from the FT platform list.
Porting thresholds and cost thresholds are configurable only through declared FREEDOM keys.
Platform adapters contain zero business logic and call canonical services through queue fabric events.
Platform simulator runs with local mocks and zero cloud credentials.
Pipeline checkpoints are persisted before the next step begins.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Tenant scope remains automatic through AsyncLocalStorage.
Store-before-event ordering and CloudEvents envelopes remain mandatory.

machineLockedRules: portingCandidate classification is MACHINE-owned
feature-manifest schema v2.0 validation precedes storeDocument
FT-ID uniqueness is checked before registry writes
signal mode is derived from ftRecord.platforms
MODE_A and MODE_B formula routing remains fixed
cost estimation never runs for portingCandidate=false
RAG retrieval includes target platform constraints
cost, effort days, and complexity score are all emitted
PROHIBITED guard executes first
terminal decisions stop the pipeline
decision record is stored before decision event emission
adapter generation refuses portingCandidate=false
adapter contains zero business logic
canonical service calls happen through queue fabric events
simulator uses local mocks only
cloud credentials are never required
implemented status is set only after simulator PASS
pipeline step order remains fixed
checkpoints are persisted before the next step
terminal steps stop the pipeline
services.note: The orchestration contract is registered in the engine-contract layer; current runtime implementation is covered by the decision, adapter, simulator, and scanner services.

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-36 values may tune module naming, acceptance thresholds, run limits, cost ceilings, retry and timeout budgets, review windows, and default target platform without changing FT identity, portingCandidate ownership, prohibited-guard order, tenant isolation, fabric boundaries, adapter purity, simulator locality, or event ordering.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to registry module naming, feature extraction acceptance thresholds, porting threshold and cost policy, adapter retry budget, simulator/pipeline timeouts, review windows, and a default target platform.

edgeCases.name: Acme feature porting control
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A tunes FLOW-36 for a stricter platform governance console with high confidence requirements, faster simulator checks, and a lower cost ceiling.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: module naming, extraction confidence, porting threshold, cost ceiling, simulator timeout
edgeCases.currentImplementation: FT identity, portingCandidate classification, prohibited-guard order, adapter purity, and tenant isolation remain fixed.

edgeCases.name: Northwind platform readiness hub
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant B tunes the package for broader marketplace readiness scanning with more FT records per extraction, a larger cost ceiling, and extra adapter retry rounds.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: extraction run size, porting threshold, cost ceiling, adapter retry budget, review window
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Cost estimation still runs only for portable features and adapters still contain zero business logic.

edgeCases.name: Tessera porting lab
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant C tunes the package for slower community review and longer simulator/pipeline timeouts while keeping a moderate porting threshold.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: review window, simulator timeout, pipeline timeout, default target platform
edgeCases.currentImplementation: No tenant can relax DNA, BFA, tenant-scope, outbox, CloudEvents, prohibited-guard, or simulator-locality rules.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T567 through T573 remain fixed.
Factory IDs F1492 through F1498 remain fixed.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
FT-ID identity remains stable across platforms.
Feature-manifest schema v2.0 validation happens before registry writes.
PROHIBITED guard runs before AI calls, cost estimation, and adapter generation.
Adapters call canonical services through queue fabric events, never direct HTTP.
Platform simulator runs with zero cloud credentials.
Pipeline checkpoints persist before the next step begins.
Terminal pipeline states stop further execution.
Tenant scope is resolved through AsyncLocalStorage.
Store-before-event ordering and CloudEvents envelopes remain mandatory.

tenantAFeaturePortingControl.description: Tenant A tunes FLOW-36 for a strict internal feature-porting control room.
tenantAFeaturePortingControl.impact: Acme gets stricter acceptance and faster feedback while feature identity, porting gates, and simulator locality remain unchanged.
tenantAFeaturePortingControl.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

tenantBReadinessHub.description: Tenant B tunes FLOW-36 for broader marketplace readiness review.
tenantBReadinessHub.impact: Northwind gets a wider readiness hub while the prohibited guard and adapter purity remain fixed.
tenantBReadinessHub.testable: Tenant B fork from Tenant A publishes a distinct package version and keeps CI green.

tenantCPortingLab.description: Tenant C tunes FLOW-36 for slower review and longer simulator windows.
tenantCPortingLab.impact: Tessera gets a community-oriented porting lab while core governance invariants stay locked.
tenantCPortingLab.testable: Tenant C fork from Tenant B adapts locally, passes package CI, and preserves auth boundaries.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-36 configuration values or display/module metadata.
FLOW-36 package assembly copies the feature-registry implementation under the canonical package slug path.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
